text: Why does Tazieff risk his life like this?
zh: Tazieff为什么要这样冒生命危险?
zh_hant: Tazieff為什麼要這樣冒生命危險?

text: Haroun Tazieff, the Polish scientist, has spent his lifetime studying active volcanoes and deep caves in all parts of the world.
zh: 波兰科学家Haroun Tazieff一生都在研究世界各地的活火山和深洞。
zh_hant: 波蘭科學家Haroun Tazieff一生都在研究世界各地的活火山和深洞。

text: In 1948, he went to Lake Kivu in the Congo to observe a new volcano which he later named Kituro.
zh: 1948年,他前往刚果的基伍湖,观察一座后来他命名为Kituro的新火山。
zh_hant: 1948年,他前往剛果的基伍湖,觀察一座後來他命名為Kituro的新火山。

text: Tazieff was able to set up his camp very close to the volcano while it was erupting violently.
zh: Tazieff得以在火山猛烈喷发时,把营地扎在离火山很近的地方。
zh_hant: Tazieff得以在火山猛烈噴發時,把營地紮在離火山很近的地方。

text: Though he managed to take a number of brilliant photographs, he could not stay near the volcano for very long.
zh: 尽管他拍到了不少精彩的照片,却无法在火山附近久留。
zh_hant: 儘管他拍到了不少精彩的照片,卻無法在火山附近久留。

text: He noticed that a river of liquid rock was coming towards him.
zh: 他注意到一条熔岩之河正朝他涌来。
zh_hant: 他注意到一條熔岩之河正朝他湧來。

text: It threatened to surround him completely, but Tazieff managed to escape just in time.
zh: 熔岩眼看就要把他团团围住,幸好Tazieff及时逃脱。
zh_hant: 熔岩眼看就要把他團團圍住,幸好Tazieff及時逃脫。

text: He waited until the volcano became quiet and he was able to return two days later.
zh: 他一直等到火山平静下来,两天后才得以返回。
zh_hant: 他一直等到火山平靜下來,兩天後才得以返回。

text: This time, he managed to climb into the mouth of Kituro so that he could take photographs and measure temperatures.
zh: 这一次,他成功爬进了Kituro的火山口,拍摄照片并测量温度。
zh_hant: 這一次,他成功爬進了Kituro的火山口,拍攝照片並測量溫度。

text: Tazieff has often risked his life in this way.
zh: Tazieff常常这样冒着生命危险。
zh_hant: Tazieff常常這樣冒著生命危險。

text: He has been able to tell us more about active volcanoes than any man alive.
zh: 他向我们讲述的关于活火山的情况,比在世的任何人都多。
zh_hant: 他向我們講述的關於活火山的情況,比在世的任何人都多。
